text: The surgeon identifies the internal opening of the fistula, potentially using hydrogen peroxide for visualization. Gentle probing delineates the tract, with care taken to follow the pathological path. A speculum may be inserted into the anal canal to facilitate probing, and an umbrella handle-shaped probe is used to align Morgagni's crypt with the fistula tract.

The surgeon palpates to identify the intersphincteric groove and opens it using diathermia. The intersphincteric space is developed, and the intersphincteric fistula tract is identified. The fistula tract is encircled and ligated with simple ligations, followed by division of the tract.

The surgeon performs a transanal advancement flap repair, beginning with a core-out fistulectomy up to the external anal sphincter. The internal opening is enlarged, and the remaining fistula is removed. The outlines of the flap are marked, and a partial thickness flap is raised using a harmonic scalpel, consisting of mucosa, submucosa, and superficial circular muscular fibers. The internal opening is closed with interrupted vicryl sutures, and the flap is secured with two barbed sutures.
